course.title: Psychiatry
course.subtitle: Mind, brain, person, society
course.description: A rights-aware, evidence-centred common academic graph spanning person-centred reasoning, medicine, neuroscience, psychotherapy, treatment, lifespan, systems, culture, and scholarship.
course.disclaimer: Academic study and simulation only. This graph is not a residency, clinical placement, licence, board-eligibility route, treatment guide, or authorization to practise psychiatry.

scope.included: The fixed 1,440-hour common academic curriculum across PSY-101 through PSY-460.
scope.excluded: The selected 120–240-hour area of depth and all real-patient, workplace-entrustment, licensure, and jurisdiction-specific authorization requirements.
scope.stabilityRule: After merge, topic IDs are stable public identifiers. Renaming or restructuring requires an explicit migration map; IDs are never silently reused for a different outcome.

linkTypes.contains: Parent hierarchy: stage to module, or module to atomic outcome.
linkTypes.prerequisite: Source should be learned before target; these links form a directed acyclic graph.
linkTypes.cross-link: A symmetric conceptual connection; it does not impose study order.

requirementLegend.common-academic: Part of the 1,440-hour common academic curriculum for every route.
requirementLegend.clinical-host: Requires supervised workplace evidence from an independently authorized clinical host and is not claimed by this graph.
requirementLegend.jurisdiction-local: Requires current local law, policy, culture, or regulator authority and cannot be made universal.
requirementLegend.area-of-depth: Belongs to one selected 120–240-hour extension and is outside this common graph.

levelLegend.stage-4-integration: Rights, systems, scholarship, and independent integration.

schema.routeResolutionRule: All three learner routes study the same common academic outcomes; route differences concern prerequisites, evidence context, and authority outside this graph.

pathways.title: Open scholar
pathways.description: Academic psychiatry for advanced learners without a claim of clinical authority.

pathways.title: Physician learner
pathways.description: Academic outcomes used alongside an independently recognized clinical program.

pathways.title: Educator or system learner
pathways.description: Curriculum, evidence, teaching, quality, and service-design study without clinical privileges.

sources.title: ACGME Program Requirements for Graduate Medical Education in Psychiatry
Royal College of Psychiatrists Core Psychiatry Curriculum
Royal College Psychiatry Training Experiences v2.0
UEMS European Training Requirements for Psychiatry
WPA High Quality Post-Graduate Training in Psychiatry
WHO QualityRights guidance and training tools
WHO/OHCHR Mental health, human rights and legislation

nodes.title: Psychiatry's field and boundaries
nodes.label: Psychiatry's field and boundaries
nodes.summary: Explain how psychiatry studies mind, brain, person, relationship, culture, and system while distinguishing academic learning from clinical authority.
nodes.outcome: Explain how psychiatry studies mind, brain, person, relationship, culture, and system while distinguishing academic learning from clinical authority.
nodes.subject: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

nodes.title: Description, inference, and interpretation
nodes.label: Description, inference, and interpretation
nodes.summary: Distinguish a person's report, another person's report, direct observation, inference, classification, and causal explanation.
nodes.outcome: Distinguish a person's report, another person's report, direct observation, inference, classification, and causal explanation.
nodes.subject: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

nodes.title: Competing explanatory models
nodes.label: Competing explanatory models
nodes.summary: Compare biological, psychological, relational, social, cultural, and disability models by the questions they answer, evidence they use, and limits they retain.
nodes.outcome: Compare biological, psychological, relational, social, cultural, and disability models by the questions they answer, evidence they use, and limits they retain.
nodes.subject: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

nodes.title: History, power, and rights
nodes.label: History, power, and rights
nodes.summary: Analyze how institutions, racism, colonialism, gender, sexuality, stigma, and survivor movements have shaped psychiatric knowledge and care.
nodes.outcome: Analyze how institutions, racism, colonialism, gender, sexuality, stigma, and survivor movements have shaped psychiatric knowledge and care.
nodes.subject: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

nodes.title: Person, recovery, and classification
nodes.label: Person, recovery, and classification
nodes.summary: Evaluate useful and harmful uses of classification while preserving personhood, strengths, neurodiversity, recovery goals, and the right to participate in decisions.
nodes.outcome: Evaluate useful and harmful uses of classification while preserving personhood, strengths, neurodiversity, recovery goals, and the right to participate in decisions.
nodes.subject: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

nodes.title: Cells, circuits, and networks
nodes.label: Cells, circuits, and networks
nodes.summary: Relate cellular signalling, circuits, large-scale networks, and behaviour without treating one scale as a complete explanation.
nodes.outcome: Relate cellular signalling, circuits, large-scale networks, and behaviour without treating one scale as a complete explanation.
nodes.subject: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

nodes.title: Neural signalling and plasticity
nodes.label: Neural signalling and plasticity
nodes.summary: Explain synaptic signalling, neuromodulation, learning, and plasticity with explicit timescale and causal limits.
nodes.outcome: Explain synaptic signalling, neuromodulation, learning, and plasticity with explicit timescale and causal limits.
nodes.subject: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

nodes.title: Development, genetics, and epigenetics
nodes.label: Development, genetics, and epigenetics
nodes.summary: Appraise how development, genetic variation, and environmentally responsive regulation contribute probabilistically rather than deterministically.
nodes.outcome: Appraise how development, genetic variation, and environmentally responsive regulation contribute probabilistically rather than deterministically.
nodes.subject: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

nodes.title: Stress, endocrine, immune, and metabolic systems
nodes.label: Stress, endocrine, immune, and metabolic systems
nodes.summary: Construct a bounded model of interacting stress, endocrine, immune, and metabolic processes and identify where clinical claims exceed evidence.
nodes.outcome: Construct a bounded model of interacting stress, endocrine, immune, and metabolic processes and identify where clinical claims exceed evidence.
nodes.subject: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

nodes.title: Sleep and circadian biology
nodes.label: Sleep and circadian biology
nodes.summary: Explain sleep regulation and circadian timing and predict how disruption can affect cognition, emotion, and physical health.
nodes.outcome: Explain sleep regulation and circadian timing and predict how disruption can affect cognition, emotion, and physical health.
nodes.subject: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

nodes.title: Neuropsychological systems
nodes.label: Neuropsychological systems
nodes.summary: Use attention, memory, language, executive, social-cognitive, and motor systems to interpret a fictional pattern of strengths and difficulties.
nodes.outcome: Use attention, memory, language, executive, social-cognitive, and motor systems to interpret a fictional pattern of strengths and difficulties.
nodes.subject: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

nodes.title: Neuroscience methods and causal inference
nodes.label: Neuroscience methods and causal inference
nodes.summary: Compare lesion, stimulation, electrophysiology, imaging, genetic, pharmacologic, and computational evidence by the inferences each design can support.
nodes.outcome: Compare lesion, stimulation, electrophysiology, imaging, genetic, pharmacologic, and computational evidence by the inferences each design can support.
nodes.subject: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

nodes.title: Mind–brain model integration
nodes.label: Mind–brain model integration
nodes.summary: Build and critique a multi-level mind–brain account that states entities, relations, assumptions, predictions, and unresolved alternatives.
nodes.outcome: Build and critique a multi-level mind–brain account that states entities, relations, assumptions, predictions, and unresolved alternatives.
nodes.subject: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

nodes.title: Developmental principles
nodes.label: Developmental principles
nodes.summary: Explain continuity, change, timing, plasticity, and person–environment transactions across the life course.
nodes.outcome: Explain continuity, change, timing, plasticity, and person–environment transactions across the life course.
nodes.subject: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

nodes.title: Attachment and caregiving
nodes.label: Attachment and caregiving
nodes.summary: Compare attachment and caregiving models while separating observed relationship patterns from deterministic predictions.
nodes.outcome: Compare attachment and caregiving models while separating observed relationship patterns from deterministic predictions.
nodes.subject: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

nodes.title: Cognition, language, and identity
nodes.label: Cognition, language, and identity
nodes.summary: Trace interacting changes in cognition, language, emotion, identity, and moral understanding across development.
nodes.outcome: Trace interacting changes in cognition, language, emotion, identity, and moral understanding across development.
nodes.subject: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

nodes.title: Adversity, protection, and resilience
nodes.label: Adversity, protection, and resilience
nodes.summary: Evaluate evidence about adversity and protection without treating exposure as destiny or resilience as an individual obligation.
nodes.outcome: Evaluate evidence about adversity and protection without treating exposure as destiny or resilience as an individual obligation.
nodes.subject: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

nodes.title: Family, peers, culture, and institutions
nodes.label: Family, peers, culture, and institutions
nodes.summary: Analyze how family, peers, education, work, migration, culture, and structural conditions shape developmental opportunities.
nodes.outcome: Analyze how family, peers, education, work, migration, culture, and structural conditions shape developmental opportunities.
nodes.subject: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

nodes.title: Life-course formulation
nodes.label: Life-course formulation
nodes.summary: Revise a fictional formulation across four ages while preserving uncertainty, strengths, turning points, and cohort context.
nodes.outcome: Revise a fictional formulation across four ages while preserving uncertainty, strengths, turning points, and cohort context.
nodes.subject: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

nodes.title: Setting, role, and consent
nodes.label: Setting, role, and consent
nodes.summary: Prepare an accessible simulated encounter by clarifying role, purpose, privacy, consent, communication needs, and limits.
nodes.outcome: Prepare an accessible simulated encounter by clarifying role, purpose, privacy, consent, communication needs, and limits.
nodes.subject: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

nodes.title: Open and focused inquiry
nodes.label: Open and focused inquiry
nodes.summary: Move from an open invitation to focused questions without replacing the person's narrative with a checklist.
nodes.outcome: Move from an open invitation to focused questions without replacing the person's narrative with a checklist.
nodes.subject: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

nodes.title: Chronology, function, strengths, and goals
nodes.label: Chronology, function, strengths, and goals
nodes.summary: Construct a time course that connects experiences with function, strengths, priorities, context, and change from baseline.
nodes.outcome: Construct a time course that connects experiences with function, strengths, priorities, context, and change from baseline.
nodes.subject: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

nodes.title: Mental-status observation
nodes.label: Mental-status observation
nodes.summary: Describe a time-bound mental-status examination while separating observation from inference and avoiding dehumanizing shorthand.
nodes.outcome: Describe a time-bound mental-status examination while separating observation from inference and avoiding dehumanizing shorthand.
nodes.subject: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

nodes.title: Communication access and interpreters
nodes.label: Communication access and interpreters
nodes.summary: Adapt a simulated interview for language, sensory, cognitive, developmental, and communication differences using appropriate support.
nodes.outcome: Adapt a simulated interview for language, sensory, cognitive, developmental, and communication differences using appropriate support.
nodes.subject: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

nodes.title: Alliance, rupture, and repair
nodes.label: Alliance, rupture, and repair
nodes.summary: Recognize a simulated relational rupture, name its possible contribution, and attempt an accountable repair.
nodes.outcome: Recognize a simulated relational rupture, name its possible contribution, and attempt an accountable repair.
nodes.subject: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

nodes.title: Boundaries and documentation
nodes.label: Boundaries and documentation
nodes.summary: Produce respectful documentation that is necessary, source-labelled, uncertainty-aware, and protected against inappropriate disclosure.
nodes.outcome: Produce respectful documentation that is necessary, source-labelled, uncertainty-aware, and protected against inappropriate disclosure.
nodes.subject: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

nodes.title: Feedback and calibration
nodes.label: Feedback and calibration
nodes.summary: Use transcript annotation, observer feedback, and self-assessment to identify one communication strength, one error, and a specific next practice target.
nodes.outcome: Use transcript annotation, observer feedback, and self-assessment to identify one communication strength, one error, and a specific next practice target.
nodes.subject: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

nodes.title: Frequency, burden, and service use
nodes.label: Frequency, burden, and service use
nodes.summary: Interpret incidence, prevalence, burden, and service-use estimates with their population, period, sampling, and measurement limits.
nodes.outcome: Interpret incidence, prevalence, burden, and service-use estimates with their population, period, sampling, and measurement limits.
nodes.subject: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

nodes.title: Measurement, validity, and invariance
nodes.label: Measurement, validity, and invariance
nodes.summary: Evaluate reliability, validity, responsiveness, measurement invariance, missing data, and instrument rights for a proposed measure.
nodes.outcome: Evaluate reliability, validity, responsiveness, measurement invariance, missing data, and instrument rights for a proposed measure.
nodes.subject: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

nodes.title: Diagnostic and prognostic evidence
nodes.label: Diagnostic and prognostic evidence
nodes.summary: Interpret sensitivity, specificity, predictive values, likelihood ratios, calibration, and decision thresholds in context.
nodes.outcome: Interpret sensitivity, specificity, predictive values, likelihood ratios, calibration, and decision thresholds in context.
nodes.subject: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

nodes.title: Study designs and causal claims
nodes.label: Study designs and causal claims
nodes.summary: Compare trials, observational studies, qualitative research, natural experiments, and causal diagrams by the questions they can answer.
nodes.outcome: Compare trials, observational studies, qualitative research, natural experiments, and causal diagrams by the questions they can answer.
nodes.subject: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

nodes.title: Effects, harms, and heterogeneity
nodes.label: Effects, harms, and heterogeneity
nodes.summary: Appraise absolute effects, uncertainty, clinical importance, heterogeneity, adverse outcomes, conflicts, and selective reporting.
nodes.outcome: Appraise absolute effects, uncertainty, clinical importance, heterogeneity, adverse outcomes, conflicts, and selective reporting.
nodes.subject: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

nodes.title: Measurement-informed learning and care
nodes.label: Measurement-informed learning and care
nodes.summary: Design a fictional longitudinal measurement plan that tracks symptoms, function, quality of life, physical health, harms, preference, and equity without surrendering judgment to a score.
nodes.outcome: Design a fictional longitudinal measurement plan that tracks symptoms, function, quality of life, physical health, harms, preference, and equity without surrendering judgment to a score.
nodes.subject: Stage I · Person, mind, brain, and method

nodes.title: Problem representation
nodes.label: Problem representation
nodes.summary: Build a concise, source-labelled problem representation that preserves time course, function, context, strengths, and uncertainty.
nodes.outcome: Build a concise, source-labelled problem representation that preserves time course, function, context, strengths, and uncertainty.
nodes.subject: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

nodes.title: Classification literacy
nodes.label: Classification literacy
nodes.summary: Use ICD and DSM-related terminology critically without reproducing proprietary criteria or treating a code as a person.
nodes.outcome: Use ICD and DSM-related terminology critically without reproducing proprietary criteria or treating a code as a person.
nodes.subject: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

nodes.title: Differential diagnosis and Bayesian updating
nodes.label: Differential diagnosis and Bayesian updating
nodes.summary: Construct and reprioritize medical, neurologic, substance-related, developmental, psychiatric, and contextual alternatives as evidence changes.
nodes.outcome: Construct and reprioritize medical, neurologic, substance-related, developmental, psychiatric, and contextual alternatives as evidence changes.
nodes.subject: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

nodes.title: Causal and maintenance formulation
nodes.label: Causal and maintenance formulation
nodes.summary: Connect predisposing, precipitating, perpetuating, protective, and structural factors in a revisable causal or maintenance model.
nodes.outcome: Connect predisposing, precipitating, perpetuating, protective, and structural factors in a revisable causal or maintenance model.
nodes.subject: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

nodes.title: Culture, strengths, and meaning
nodes.label: Culture, strengths, and meaning
nodes.summary: Integrate explanatory models, identity, spirituality, culture, power, goals, and strengths without stereotyping or diagnostic overshadowing.
nodes.outcome: Integrate explanatory models, identity, spirituality, culture, power, goals, and strengths without stereotyping or diagnostic overshadowing.
nodes.subject: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

nodes.title: Communicating and updating a formulation
nodes.label: Communicating and updating a formulation
nodes.summary: Explain a working formulation collaboratively, state confidence and alternatives, invite correction, and define evidence that would prompt revision.
nodes.outcome: Explain a working formulation collaboratively, state confidence and alternatives, invite correction, and define evidence that would prompt revision.
nodes.subject: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

nodes.title: Medical and neurologic alternatives
nodes.label: Medical and neurologic alternatives
nodes.summary: Generate prioritized medical, neurologic, medication, substance, sleep, and physiologic alternatives for a fictional psychiatric presentation.
nodes.outcome: Generate prioritized medical, neurologic, medication, substance, sleep, and physiologic alternatives for a fictional psychiatric presentation.
nodes.subject: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

nodes.title: Integrated history and medication reconciliation
nodes.label: Integrated history and medication reconciliation
nodes.summary: Organize medical, neurologic, developmental, medication, substance, reproductive, sleep, and family information with source and chronology.
nodes.outcome: Organize medical, neurologic, developmental, medication, substance, reproductive, sleep, and family information with source and chronology.
nodes.subject: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

nodes.title: Examination and investigations
nodes.label: Examination and investigations
nodes.summary: Select and interpret bounded physical, neurologic, cognitive, laboratory, and imaging information while avoiding indiscriminate testing.
nodes.outcome: Select and interpret bounded physical, neurologic, cognitive, laboratory, and imaging information while avoiding indiscriminate testing.
nodes.subject: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

nodes.title: Delirium and cognitive change
nodes.label: Delirium and cognitive change
nodes.summary: Distinguish acute attention and awareness change from longer-term cognitive and psychiatric patterns and identify urgent escalation in simulation.
nodes.outcome: Distinguish acute attention and awareness change from longer-term cognitive and psychiatric patterns and identify urgent escalation in simulation.
nodes.subject: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

nodes.title: Physical health, prevention, and treatment burden
nodes.label: Physical health, prevention, and treatment burden
nodes.summary: Analyze preventable physical-health inequity, medication burden, lifestyle constraints, access, and diagnostic overshadowing.
nodes.outcome: Analyze preventable physical-health inequity, medication burden, lifestyle constraints, access, and diagnostic overshadowing.
nodes.subject: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

nodes.title: Integrated physical-health plan
nodes.label: Integrated physical-health plan
nodes.summary: Construct an academic monitoring and coordination plan that states priorities, interactions, uncertainty, roles, and when licensed care is required.
nodes.outcome: Construct an academic monitoring and coordination plan that states priorities, interactions, uncertainty, roles, and when licensed care is required.
nodes.subject: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

nodes.title: Recognizing urgency
nodes.label: Recognizing urgency
nodes.summary: Identify fictional patterns that require emergency assessment while distinguishing urgency from prediction certainty.
nodes.outcome: Identify fictional patterns that require emergency assessment while distinguishing urgency from prediction certainty.
nodes.subject: Stage II · Formulation, safety, and presentations

nodes.title: Self-harm and suicide formulation
nodes.label: Self-harm and suicide formulation
nodes.summary: Build a dynamic, person-centred safety formulation using current and changing factors, protective resources, uncertainty, and direct escalation.

nodes.label: Antipsychotic evidence and harms

nodes.subject: Stage III · Treatment, lifespan, and continuity

nodes.title: Ethics, power, and conflict
nodes.label: Ethics, power, and conflict
nodes.summary: Analyze a psychiatric dilemma using autonomy, benefit, harm, justice, relationship, power, uncertainty, conflicts, and accountability.
nodes.outcome: Analyze a psychiatric dilemma using autonomy, benefit, harm, justice, relationship, power, uncertainty, conflicts, and accountability.
nodes.subject: Stage IV · Rights, systems, and scholarship

nodes.title: Capacity, consent, and confidentiality
nodes.label: Capacity, consent, and confidentiality
nodes.summary: Explain decision-specific capacity, supported decision-making, informed consent, confidentiality, disclosure limits, and documentation with jurisdiction labels.
nodes.outcome: Explain decision-specific capacity, supported decision-making, informed consent, confidentiality, disclosure limits, and documentation with jurisdiction labels.
nodes.subject: Stage IV · Rights, systems, and scholarship

nodes.title: Mental-health law and jurisdiction
nodes.label: Mental-health law and jurisdiction
nodes.summary: Compare legal and ethical questions without presenting one jurisdiction's detention, treatment, review, safeguarding, or duty rules as universal.
nodes.outcome: Compare legal and ethical questions without presenting one jurisdiction's detention, treatment, review, safeguarding, or duty rules as universal.
nodes.subject: Stage IV · Rights, systems, and scholarship

nodes.title: Forensic questions and role boundaries
nodes.label: Forensic questions and role boundaries
nodes.summary: Distinguish therapeutic and forensic roles, evidence standards, risk uncertainty, report duties, bias, and the limits of academic simulation.
nodes.outcome: Distinguish therapeutic and forensic roles, evidence standards, risk uncertainty, report duties, bias, and the limits of academic simulation.
nodes.subject: Stage IV · Rights, systems, and scholarship

nodes.title: Human rights and coercion reduction
nodes.label: Human rights and coercion reduction
nodes.summary: Evaluate supported decisions, advance planning, community inclusion, restraint and seclusion reduction, complaint routes, and accountable alternatives to coercion.
nodes.outcome: Evaluate supported decisions, advance planning, community inclusion, restraint and seclusion reduction, complaint routes, and accountable alternatives to coercion.
nodes.subject: Stage IV · Rights, systems, and scholarship

nodes.title: Cultural humility and explanatory models
nodes.label: Cultural humility and explanatory models
nodes.summary: Elicit and compare explanatory models, language, identity, values, and healing practices while examining the learner's own assumptions.
nodes.outcome: Elicit and compare explanatory models, language, identity, values, and healing practices while examining the learner's own assumptions.
nodes.subject: Stage IV · Rights, systems, and scholarship

nodes.title: Racism, colonialism, and structural conditions
nodes.label: Racism, colonialism, and structural conditions
nodes.summary: Analyze how racism, colonization, migration policy, poverty, housing, education, policing, and services shape exposure, interpretation, access, and outcome.
nodes.outcome: Analyze how racism, colonization, migration policy, poverty, housing, education, policing, and services shape exposure, interpretation, access, and outcome.
nodes.subject: Stage IV · Rights, systems, and scholarship

nodes.title: Identity, disability, and neurodiversity
nodes.label: Identity, disability, and neurodiversity
nodes.summary: Integrate gender, sexuality, disability, neurodiversity, age, class, and intersectionality without treating identity as pathology or a checklist.
nodes.outcome: Integrate gender, sexuality, disability, neurodiversity, age, class, and intersectionality without treating identity as pathology or a checklist.
nodes.subject: Stage IV · Rights, systems, and scholarship

nodes.title: Spirituality, language, and migration
nodes.label: Spirituality, language, and migration
nodes.summary: Discuss spirituality, religion, idioms of distress, interpreters, migration, exile, and belonging with curiosity, consent, and differential awareness.
nodes.outcome: Discuss spirituality, religion, idioms of distress, interpreters, migration, exile, and belonging with curiosity, consent, and differential awareness.
nodes.subject: Stage IV · Rights, systems, and scholarship

nodes.title: Culturally safe formulation and action
nodes.label: Culturally safe formulation and action
nodes.summary: Revise a fictional formulation and service plan with community authority, cultural safety, structural action, communication access, and a test for unintended harm.
nodes.outcome: Revise a fictional formulation and service plan with community authority, cultural safety, structural action, communication access, and a test for unintended harm.
nodes.subject: Stage IV · Rights, systems, and scholarship

nodes.title: Safety science and learning systems
nodes.label: Safety science and learning systems
nodes.summary: Use systems thinking to analyze a fictional adverse event without reducing causes to individual blame.
nodes.outcome: Use systems thinking to analyze a fictional adverse event without reducing causes to individual blame.
nodes.subject: Stage IV · Rights, systems, and scholarship

nodes.title: Quality measures and process mapping
nodes.label: Quality measures and process mapping
nodes.summary: Define an aim, population, process, outcome, balancing measures, run-chart logic, and data-quality limits for an improvement problem.
nodes.outcome: Define an aim, population, process, outcome, balancing measures, run-chart logic, and data-quality limits for an improvement problem.
nodes.subject: Stage IV · Rights, systems, and scholarship

nodes.title: Implementation and de-implementation
nodes.label: Implementation and de-implementation
nodes.summary: Select implementation strategies from identified barriers and mechanisms and define conditions for adaptation, de-implementation, or stopping.
nodes.outcome: Select implementation strategies from identified barriers and mechanisms and define conditions for adaptation, de-implementation, or stopping.
nodes.subject: Stage IV · Rights, systems, and scholarship

nodes.title: Co-design, equity, and governance
nodes.label: Co-design, equity, and governance
nodes.summary: Design stakeholder governance that gives affected people real authority, compensation, accessibility, privacy, conflict disclosure, and equity measures.
nodes.outcome: Design stakeholder governance that gives affected people real authority, compensation, accessibility, privacy, conflict disclosure, and equity measures.
nodes.subject: Stage IV · Rights, systems, and scholarship

nodes.title: Evaluation, spread, and stopping rules
nodes.label: Evaluation, spread, and stopping rules
nodes.summary: Construct an ethical evaluation with comparison, uncertainty, harms, resource effects, sustainability, and explicit rules to stop or reverse ineffective practice.
nodes.outcome: Construct an ethical evaluation with comparison, uncertainty, harms, resource effects, sustainability, and explicit rules to stop or reverse ineffective practice.
nodes.subject: Stage IV · Rights, systems, and scholarship

nodes.title: Telepsychiatry and hybrid care
nodes.label: Telepsychiatry and hybrid care
nodes.summary: Evaluate telepsychiatry for access, communication, privacy, safety, exclusion, emergency limits, licensing, workflow, and equivalent non-digital routes.
nodes.outcome: Evaluate telepsychiatry for access, communication, privacy, safety, exclusion, emergency limits, licensing, workflow, and equivalent non-digital routes.
nodes.subject: Stage IV · Rights, systems, and scholarship

nodes.title: Digital measures and phenotyping
nodes.label: Digital measures and phenotyping
nodes.summary: Appraise passive and active digital measures for construct validity, consent, missingness, surveillance, context, bias, drift, and clinical usefulness.
nodes.outcome: Appraise passive and active digital measures for construct validity, consent, missingness, surveillance, context, bias, drift, and clinical usefulness.
nodes.subject: Stage IV · Rights, systems, and scholarship

nodes.title: AI evaluation and human factors
nodes.label: AI evaluation and human factors
nodes.summary: Evaluate a psychiatric AI claim using task definition, data provenance, subgroup performance, calibration, explainability, workflow, automation bias, and failure modes.
nodes.outcome: Evaluate a psychiatric AI claim using task definition, data provenance, subgroup performance, calibration, explainability, workflow, automation bias, and failure modes.
nodes.subject: Stage IV · Rights, systems, and scholarship

nodes.title: Privacy, security, and refusal conditions
nodes.label: Privacy, security, and refusal conditions
nodes.summary: Design governance for data minimization, access, security, monitoring, incident response, withdrawal, accountability, and a defensible decision not to deploy.
nodes.outcome: Design governance for data minimization, access, security, monitoring, incident response, withdrawal, accountability, and a defensible decision not to deploy.
nodes.subject: Stage IV · Rights, systems, and scholarship

nodes.title: Team leadership and collaboration
nodes.label: Team leadership and collaboration
nodes.summary: Lead a simulated multidisciplinary discussion that clarifies purpose, roles, disagreement, patient participation, evidence, decision, and follow-up.
nodes.outcome: Lead a simulated multidisciplinary discussion that clarifies purpose, roles, disagreement, patient participation, evidence, decision, and follow-up.
nodes.subject: Stage IV · Rights, systems, and scholarship

nodes.title: Teaching and assessment
nodes.label: Teaching and assessment
nodes.summary: Design a short teaching activity with a learner model, observable outcome, guided practice, feedback, transfer, accessibility, and valid evidence of learning.
nodes.outcome: Design a short teaching activity with a learner model, observable outcome, guided practice, feedback, transfer, accessibility, and valid evidence of learning.
nodes.subject: Stage IV · Rights, systems, and scholarship

nodes.title: Supervision and feedback
nodes.label: Supervision and feedback
nodes.summary: Prepare for supervision, present uncertainty, seek help, give task-focused feedback, respond to challenge, and define what must remain with an authorized supervisor.
nodes.outcome: Prepare for supervision, present uncertainty, seek help, give task-focused feedback, respond to challenge, and define what must remain with an authorized supervisor.
nodes.subject: Stage IV · Rights, systems, and scholarship

nodes.title: Boundaries, wellbeing, and impairment
nodes.label: Boundaries, wellbeing, and impairment
nodes.summary: Analyze workload, moral injury, boundaries, fatigue, impairment, mistreatment, help-seeking, and organizational responsibility without making resilience an individual cure.
nodes.outcome: Analyze workload, moral injury, boundaries, fatigue, impairment, mistreatment, help-seeking, and organizational responsibility without making resilience an individual cure.
nodes.subject: Stage IV · Rights, systems, and scholarship

nodes.title: Conflict, error, and repair
nodes.label: Conflict, error, and repair
nodes.summary: Respond to a simulated conflict or error with disclosure, immediate protection, apology where appropriate, investigation, learning, support, and accountable repair.
nodes.outcome: Respond to a simulated conflict or error with disclosure, immediate protection, apology where appropriate, investigation, learning, support, and accountable repair.
nodes.subject: Stage IV · Rights, systems, and scholarship

nodes.title: Integrated case reasoning
nodes.label: Integrated case reasoning
nodes.summary: Construct and revise a complex fictional formulation spanning person, brain and body, development, relationships, culture, systems, safety, and uncertainty.
nodes.outcome: Construct and revise a complex fictional formulation spanning person, brain and body, development, relationships, culture, systems, safety, and uncertainty.
nodes.subject: Stage IV · Rights, systems, and scholarship

nodes.title: Independent evidence synthesis
nodes.label: Independent evidence synthesis
nodes.summary: Answer a bounded psychiatric question using transparent search, critical appraisal, absolute effects, harms, lived-experience evidence, applicability, rights, and uncertainty.
nodes.outcome: Answer a bounded psychiatric question using transparent search, critical appraisal, absolute effects, harms, lived-experience evidence, applicability, rights, and uncertainty.
nodes.subject: Stage IV · Rights, systems, and scholarship

nodes.title: Service and rights design
nodes.label: Service and rights design
nodes.summary: Design a rights-based service response with co-governance, implementation theory, quality measures, equity analysis, privacy, cost, and stopping rules.
nodes.outcome: Design a rights-based service response with co-governance, implementation theory, quality measures, equity analysis, privacy, cost, and stopping rules.
nodes.subject: Stage IV · Rights, systems, and scholarship

nodes.title: Teaching and public explanation
nodes.label: Teaching and public explanation
nodes.summary: Teach a difficult psychiatric idea to a declared audience using accurate, accessible, non-stigmatizing explanation and respond to uncertainty and disagreement.
nodes.outcome: Teach a difficult psychiatric idea to a declared audience using accurate, accessible, non-stigmatizing explanation and respond to uncertainty and disagreement.
nodes.subject: Stage IV · Rights, systems, and scholarship

nodes.title: Portfolio, calibration, and limits
nodes.label: Portfolio, calibration, and limits
nodes.summary: Defend a portfolio showing retrieval, transfer, feedback uptake, confidence calibration, corrections, help-seeking, and the boundary between academic and clinical evidence.
nodes.outcome: Defend a portfolio showing retrieval, transfer, feedback uptake, confidence calibration, corrections, help-seeking, and the boundary between academic and clinical evidence.
nodes.subject: Stage IV · Rights, systems, and scholarship

nodes.title: Capstone defence
nodes.label: Capstone defence
nodes.summary: Defend an independently authored capstone before academic, clinical, lived-experience, rights, and systems perspectives and resolve every material correction.
nodes.outcome: Defend an independently authored capstone before academic, clinical, lived-experience, rights, and systems perspectives and resolve every material correction.
nodes.subject: Stage IV · Rights, systems, and scholarship
